hooks.command: d="$PWD"; while [ "$d" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$d/.claude/hooks/check-dotfiles-sync.sh" ]; do d="$(dirname "$d")"; done; bash "$d/.claude/hooks/check-dotfiles-sync.sh"

hooks.prompt: A git push just completed. Monitor the CI run: 1) Run `gh run list --limit 1 --json databaseId,status,workflowName,headBranch` to find the triggered run. 2) If a run was triggered, run `gh run watch <id> --exit-status` in background to monitor it. 3) When notified of completion, if it failed: run `gh run view <id> --log-failed` to get failure details, then diagnose and fix the issue. If it passed, briefly report success. Skip monitoring if the push was to a repo without CI (e.g. docs/research/).

hooks.command: d="$PWD"; while [ "$d" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$d/.claude/hooks/stop_validation.py" ]; do d="$(dirname "$d")"; done; python3 "$d/.claude/hooks/stop_validation.py"
